message:
fix(tui): handle empty cells in buffer rendering and normalize frame rect origin

Fix two related TUI rendering issues:

1. Empty cell handling (app.rs, widgets.rs):
   When converting an ftui Buffer to a string, cells with empty content
   (no character, no grapheme, not a continuation) were silently skipped.
   This caused columns to collapse together in the rendered output, breaking
   alignment for widgets that use empty cells as whitespace. Now empty cells
   emit a space character, preserving the intended visual layout.

2. Frame rect normalization (test_harness.rs):
   The test harness render_to_string helper was passing the raw area rect
   (which may have non-zero x,y origin) directly to the draw function, but
   the Frame buffer always starts at (0,0). This caused the draw function
   to write outside the buffer bounds or to the wrong positions when the
   area had a non-zero origin. Normalize the rect to (0,0,width,height)
   before passing to the draw callback.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
